strings: m_c started a huge argument between multi_cat.
m_c can't stand to see r_c1 and r_c2 together.
m_c fantasizes about r_c1 ending {PRONOUN/r_c1/poss} relationship with r_c2.
m_c feels both guilty and happy at the thought of something happening to r_c2 so r_c1 will move on.
m_c has a dream where r_c2 never existed and r_c1 became m_c's mate instead.
m_c thinks r_c1 would be happier in a relationship with {PRONOUN/m_c/object} instead of r_c2.
m_c sees r_c1 argue with r_c2 and hopes it's a sign they'll break up.
m_c tracked a trail of muddy pawprints into the warrior's den, straight through the nests of multi_cat.
m_c returned to the warrior's den dripping mud everywhere and managed to ruin multi_cat's nests.
m_c tromped into the warrior's den with muddy paws, trailing muck straight through multi_cat's nests.
m_c tracked a trail of muddy pawprints into the elder's den, straight through the nests of multi_cat.
m_c returned to the elder's den dripping mud everywhere and managed to ruin multi_cat's nests.
m_c tromped into the elder's den with muddy paws, trailing muck straight through multi_cat's nests.
m_c tracked a trail of muddy pawprints into the apprentice's den, straight through the nests of multi_cat.
m_c returned to the apprentice's den dripping mud everywhere and managed to ruin multi_cat's nests.
m_c tromped into the apprentice's den with muddy paws, trailing muck straight through multi_cat's nests.
m_c spends the Gathering whispering to multi_cat, making it nearly impossible for anyone nearby to hear the leaders speak.
m_c won't keep {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} mouth shut during the Gathering. multi_cat can barely hear the leader's announcements!
No matter how many warning looks multi_cat send {PRONOUN/m_c/object}, m_c keeps talking over the Gathering announcements.
m_c, wanting to help keep {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} Clanmates in shape and ready for anything, wakes multi_cat up early for morning stretches.
Early in the morning, m_c rallies multi_cat to do stretches in hopes of keeping {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} Clanmates prepared for anything.
multi_cat can't escape from m_c waking them up to do early morning stretches.
multi_cat respect how dutiful m_c is to training, but they all wish they weren't up doing stretches at dawn.
multi_cat bemoan m_c's insistence that they get up and do stretches at dawn. While they do it, nobody but m_c likes it.
m_c rallies multi_cat to do early morning stretches with {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.
multi_cat wish that m_c let them sleep instead of getting them up for early morning stretches.
Stretching might be good for one's health, but multi_cat wish m_c didn't insist on stretching at the crack of dawn.
m_c, noticing the state of disarray the camp is in, gathers multi_cat to help {PRONOUN/m_c/object} clean up.
m_c recruits multi_cat to tidy up camp upon noticing how unorganized it has become.
Camp has become a disorganized mess, so m_c convinces multi_cat to help {PRONOUN/m_c/object} tidy it up.
The dens are in need of repair, so m_c drags multi_cat to help {PRONOUN/m_c/object} with repairs.
m_c pesters multi_cat into helping {PRONOUN/m_c/object} with repairs across camp.
multi_cat can respect m_c's dedication to keeping camp clean, but each cat has something else they'd rather be doing instead of cleaning.
multi_cat won't deny that camp needs to be cleaned, but they wish that m_c hadn't gathered them to do it.
Before a den comes caving in, m_c forces multi_cat to help {PRONOUN/m_c/object} with maintenance.
m_c attempts to impart {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} knowledge onto multi_cat, but none of the apprentices pays attention.
m_c tries teaching multi_cat one of {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} skills but gives up because no apprentice listens.
m_c had an informative lecture planned for multi_cat, but gives up midway because the apprentices ignore {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.
multi_cat will not stop talking to each other during m_c's lesson, and m_c calls it off.
m_c spent an entire day planning a lesson for multi_cat, but it goes to waste as the apprentices are distracted by each other.
m_c cannot believe how disrespectful multi_cat are while {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} {VERB/m_c/try/tries} to give a lecture.
m_c wants this training exercise to be a success, but multi_cat refuse to listen, and m_c gives up.
It is impossible to make multi_cat understand the importance of stretching properly, so m_c calls the exercise off.
multi_cat are too busy goofing off to listen to m_c's advice.
m_c attempts to keep multi_cat focused during training, but the cats continuously talk about unrelated topics.
multi_cat pointedly ignore m_c's reminders to focus on training instead of gossiping.
What begins as a petty argument between m_c and r_c1 turns into a spat involving multi_cat as well.
The argument between m_c and r_c1 spills into multi_cat's day, leading to all the cats hissing at each other.
Nobody is safe from m_c and r_c1's dispute, and multi_cat are dragged into it.
The screaming match between m_c and r_c1 soon involves multi_cat as well.
multi_cat wake up to m_c and r_c1's yowling. They give the snappy pair a piece of their minds.
Trying to eat while m_c and r_c1 scream at each other is impossible, so multi_cat decide to chip in with their opinions.
The debate over who gets to be the leader in a game explodes into a hurtful argument between m_c, multi_cat.
m_c hurls insults at multi_cat for thinking they deserve a chance to play as the leader.
m_c is appalled when multi_cat won't let {PRONOUN/m_c/object} play as the leader, so {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} {VERB/m_c/begin/begins} insulting everyone.
A fight over a toy escalates into a screaming match between m_c, multi_cat.
m_c calls multi_cat a bunch of wusses after a play-fight, and it escalates into a fight between the them all.
m_c, multi_cat can't settle who gets a toy.
m_c won't stop instigating a fight between multi_cat.
m_c orders multi_cat to give {PRONOUN/m_c/object} a toy they are playing with and starts a fight when told no.
A game of make-believe turns into a real scuffle between m_c, multi_cat when they can't decide who will play what in the next round.
m_c calls everyone in the nursery is a mouse-brain. It leads to an argument between {PRONOUN/m_c/self}, multi_cat.
m_c keeps hogging being the leader during a game, causing a fight to break out between {PRONOUN/m_c/self}, multi_cat over who gets to be it next.
m_c demands that r_c1 give up playing the leader in a game, which sparks an argument that drags multi_cat in as well.
m_c screams in r_c1 face that {PRONOUN/r_c1/subject} {VERB/r_c1/suck/sucks}, sparking an argument that snowballs and drags in both multi_cat.
m_c didn't think it would be too hard to watch the kits, but multi_cat prove that thought very, very wrong.
It hasn't been that long since m_c began kitsitting multi_cat, but chaos is already underway.
m_c wants to scream into {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} nest after kitsitting multi_cat.
m_c doesn't have a moment to rest while kitsitting with how prone to trouble multi_cat are.
While kitsitting, m_c tries — and fails — to rein in the rambunctiousness of multi_cat.
Nobody ever said kitsitting was easy, but m_c thought that multi_cat would be easier to watch than they actually are.
m_c wishes {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} could blink while kitsitting without missing a heartbeat of multi_cat's troublemaking.
After kitsitting multi_cat, m_c takes time to recover from the endless mischief.
multi_cat come to m_c with a complaint regarding patrols, but get blown off by m_c.
m_c refuses to understand multi_cat's points about patrol organization.
m_c dismisses multi_cat's arguments by reminding them who their deputy is.
When multi_cat begin an argument about hunting, m_c silences them through {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} authority.
None of multi_cat's critiques about {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} usage of authority matter to m_c.
